item.title: snapshot.ts silently swallows builder VM destroy failures — leaves orphaned instances
item.intent: No cloud resource must be silently abandoned. If a builder VM cannot be destroyed, the user must be warned with enough info to clean it up manually.
item.description: src/cloud/provisioning/snapshot.ts lines 113, 126, and 144 call adapter.destroyVm() with .catch(() => {}) — silently discarding any destroy error. If the cleanup of the temporary builder VM fails (API error, timeout, network failure), the user is never notified and the VM continues running, incurring cost. Fix: replace .catch(() => {}) with .catch((err) => { log a warning to stderr with the VM ID and provider so the user can manually destroy it }).
acceptanceCriteria: No .catch(() => {}) on destroyVm() calls in snapshot.ts
Builder VM destroy failure logs a warning to stderr with VM ID and provider context
